# RHA Section 4A — Unfair Practices & the Rental Housing Tribunal

Section 4A was introduced by the **Rental Housing Amendment Act 35 of 2014** and lists practices **deemed unfair** in the landlord–tenant relationship. Most of s4A is in force; some provisions awaited regulations from the Minister. Provincial Tribunals apply s4A robustly as the primary tenant-protection lever.

<!-- BEGIN AUTO-GENERATED legal-facts -->
<!-- Content rendered by manage.py render_legal_skills — edit YAML in content/legal/statutes/rha/ -->
<!-- END AUTO-GENERATED legal-facts -->

## 1. Unfair practices — list

Section 4A (read with the Unfair Practice Regulations, published province by province) covers:

| Practice | Rule |
|---|---|
| **Changing locks** without consent or a court order | Unfair; also may constitute spoliation |
| **Refusing to provide a written lease** when tenant requests one | Unfair under s4A + breach of s5(2) |
| **Withholding a deposit** without lawful deduction or inspection | Unfair — Tribunal routinely orders full refund |
| **Not placing the deposit** in interest-bearing account | Unfair + s5(3)(f) breach |
| **Failing to conduct joint inspection** (ingoing or outgoing) | Unfair + forfeits right to deduct |
| **Charging unauthorised fees** (admin, key, reconnection, early-termination penalties beyond CPA) | Unfair |
| **Interfering with tenant's use and enjoyment** | Unfair + breach of s4(2) privacy and s5A(e) |
| **Evicting without court order** | Unlawful eviction; criminal and civil liability |
| **Discrimination** on any s4(1) ground | Unfair |
| **Refusing reasonable repairs** | Unfair + s5A breach |
| **Disconnecting municipal services** (water, electricity) to force payment | Unfair + spoliation |
| **Unilateral rental escalation** | Unfair |
| **Tenants' Association interference** | Prohibited |
| **Threats, harassment, intimidation** | Unfair |
| **Refusing tenant access** to common areas or parking as per lease | Unfair |
| **Refusing to provide receipts** on request | Unfair + s5A(a) |

## 2. Rental Housing Tribunal — structure and jurisdiction

Each province has a **Rental Housing Tribunal** (RHT) established under s7 of the RHA.

### Powers (s13)
- Investigate and mediate rental disputes
- **Issue rulings** that have the **effect of a Magistrate's Court order** (s13(12))
- Attach property, order refunds, order repairs, order cessation of unfair practice
- Impose **fines** up to R10,000 and/or imprisonment up to 2 years for contraventions of rulings (s16)

### What the Tribunal CAN do
- Order deposit refund with interest
- Order repairs within a time period
- Order reinstatement of evicted tenant (pre-court-order "evictions")
- Order payment of arrears
- Rule on unfair practice complaints
- Mediate settlements

### What the Tribunal CANNOT do
- Grant **eviction orders** — those require the Magistrate's Court under PIE
- Award general damages (beyond specific monetary orders tied to the lease)
- Set aside leases

### Procedure (Regulations vary by province)
1. Complainant completes provincial complaint form
2. Tribunal serves complaint on respondent
3. Mediation attempt (often mandatory first step)
4. If mediation fails → formal hearing
5. Ruling issued — enforceable as court order

### Cost
**Free** to lodge a complaint. No legal representation required (though permitted).

## 3. Provincial Tribunal contacts (as of 2026)

| Province | Tribunal |
|---|---|
| Western Cape | WC Rental Housing Tribunal, Cape Town |
| Gauteng | GP Rental Housing Tribunal, Johannesburg + Pretoria |
| KwaZulu-Natal | KZN RHT, Durban + PMB |
| Eastern Cape | EC RHT, Bhisho |
| Free State | FS RHT, Bloemfontein |
| Limpopo, Mpumalanga, North West, Northern Cape | Provincial offices |

Each Tribunal publishes its own **Unfair Practices Regulations** — some are more detailed than others. Western Cape and Gauteng have the most developed jurisprudence.

## 4. Common s4A cases founders should know

| Case type | Typical outcome |
|---|---|
| Deposit withheld without inspection | Full refund + interest ordered |
| Lockout after non-payment | Reinstatement + damages for spoliation |
| Utility disconnection | Reconnection ordered, fine possible |
| Refusal to sign written lease | Written lease ordered + costs |
| Excessive deposit (> 2× rent) | Reduction or declaration of unfair practice |
| Sudden rent increase | Increase set aside |
| Refusal of emergency repairs | Repairs ordered within 7–30 days |

## 5. Defences for the landlord

The landlord can defend by showing:
- A **signed joint inspection report** documenting damage (for deposit deductions)
- **Written notice and opportunity to remedy** (for alleged breach by tenant)
- **Invoices / quotations** for repairs
- **Proof of interest-bearing account** lodgement
- **Written lease** with disputed clause
- **Municipal approval** for building work during occupation

## 6. Practical founder strategy

1. **Document everything.** The Tribunal decides on the balance of probabilities. No docs = lose.
2. **Use plain language.** Tribunal applies s5(4) + CPA s22 strictly.
3. **Respond fast.** Delay in providing receipts, refunds, or inspection reports is evidence of unfair practice.
4. **Settle early at mediation.** Formal hearings create a public record and can go badly.
5. **Track 7/14/21 deposit deadlines.** Missing these almost guarantees a s4A ruling against you.
6. **Never self-help evict or disconnect services.** Even if tenant hasn't paid for months, always go through court.

## 7. Interplay with other laws

| Statute | Link |
|---|---|
| **PIE Act 19/1998** | Eviction requires Magistrate's Court order |
| **CPA** | Fixed-term cancellation (s14), unreasonable terms (s48) |
| **Sectional Titles Schemes Management Act + CSOS Act** | Body corporate conduct rules enforceable via CSOS separately |
| **POPIA** | Unlawful tenant blacklisting / data sharing could be both a POPIA and s4A unfair practice |

## 8. Founder quick-check

| ✅ | Item |
|---|---|
| ☐ | Deposit in interest-bearing account — documentary proof on file |
| ☐ | Joint ingoing + outgoing inspection reports signed by both parties |
| ☐ | Written lease with all s5(3) contents |
| ☐ | House rules annexed |
| ☐ | Emergency repair log |
| ☐ | Rent receipts issued on request |
| ☐ | No lockouts, disconnections, or self-help remedies |
| ☐ | Escalation only as per lease, never unilateral |
| ☐ | Breach notices in writing with remedy period |
| ☐ | Evictions only via court order under PIE |
